TextENGNew: ``Excuse me, I'd like to say something before we begin the proceedings.``
The village elders immediately sensed that my raising my hand had to do with Satoko.
...They hadn't grown old for nothing, I suppose.
``Ah, Keiichi-kun.@k@vS12/18/183300017. Today is our final review session, so could you save personal matters for later?``
But I pressed on without paying him any mind.

``You could've just told me directly instead of going through Mion or Chie-sensei.@k@vS12/01/180101300. I don't appreciate the roundabout approach.@k@vS12/01/180101301. Let's just settle this face to face.``
A dead silence fell over the room.
No one wanted anything to do with a conversation involving the Houjou family.

``Everyone here knows that Satoko Houjou is suffering right now.@k@vS12/01/180101303. Fine, I won't hold it against you for not actively helping.@k@vS12/01/180101304. I'm sure plenty of you just don't want the hassle.``
``......But we're different.@k@vS12/01/180101306. Satoko is one of us.@k@vS12/01/180101307. We're prepared to risk everything to save her.@k@vS12/01/180101308. And yet--``
``Keiichi-kun.@k@vS12/18/KIMI08002. We know all about that already.@k@vS12/18/KIMI08003. What we're trying to say is that you've already pushed the consultation center hard enough...!``
``And is there some problem with pushing harder?``
``......Of course there is!@k@vS12/00/eldA18006. You bet there is!!``
Someone else spoke up in a harsh tone.
...Heh heh, looks like they finally stepped into my ring.
``Then what exactly is the problem? Could you explain it so even a kid like me can understand?``
``Listen here, that nonsense like the dam war is ancient history!@k@vS12/00/eldB18002. The days of forming mobs and storming government offices are over!``
Predictably, their reason didn't even mention the first syllable of Satoko's name.@k It wasn't a reason at all.
``I didn't ask about your generation's story, and the dam war has nothing to do with this.@k@vS12/01/180101312. This is my war!!``
``And if you're going to involve yourselves in my war, then make it clear -- are you my enemy or my ally?@k@vS12/01/180101314. Which is it?!@k@vS12/01/180101315. Are you with me, or against me?!``
Murmur murmur murmur!!@k The nerve of this boy, talking like that!@k Kids these days have no manners!!
The room erupted into chaos, the air thick with the smell of gunpowder.
But my allies -- Rena, the Coach, Shion -- held their ground with fierce gazes, unfazed by the commotion.
Their unwavering stares were worth a million cheering supporters to me.
I was merely shouting on everyone's behalf. This wasn't my fight alone.@k It was everyone's fight!
So even as the old men concentrated their hostile glares on me, I didn't flinch in the slightest.

``Keiichi-kun.@k@vS12/18/183300022. This isn't about enemies and allies.@k@vS12/18/183300023. It's true that the village fought the government during the dam war.@k@vS12/18/183300024. And yes, as you say, the dam war is no longer relevant.@k@vS12/18/183300025. That's all in the past now.``

``I'm not asking about your circumstances.@k@vS12/01/180101317. I'm talking about me, my friends, and Satoko.@k@vS12/01/180101318. I didn't ask a single thing about the dam war.@k@vS12/01/180101319. Maybe you should get your ears checked, old man.``
``You... How dare you speak to your elders that way!!@k@vS12/00/eldC18002. Don't you have any respect for your betters?!@k@vS12/00/eldC18003. I'd like to see your parents' faces!!``
Hearing that, my dad gave an embarrassed, awkward smile.
``...Excuse me.@k@vS12/00/182600052. Sorry for the awkward introduction.``
``You're his father?!@k@vS12/00/eldC18005. What kind of upbringing did you give him?!``

``Hahahaha...@k@vS12/00/182600054. As a parent, I probably fall into the failing category.@k@vS12/00/182600055. ...Whether I raised Keiichi properly -- whether I can hold my head high about it -- I honestly don't know.``
His self-deprecating words trailed off with a bitter smile -- and then his expression hardened into a sharp glare.

``However, a single man named Maebara Keiichi stands here before all of you, voicing his conviction that something is deeply wrong.@k@vS12/00/182600057. This has nothing to do with our relationship as father and son.@k@vS12/00/182600058. He is simply making his stand as one man.``
``So I don't believe my face is particularly relevant here, is it?``
Caught completely off guard by this response, the elders sat there dumbfounded.
...Ooishi-san broke the silence with a laugh.
``Seems to me you've got plenty to hold your head high about.@k@vS12/11/180700312. Nfu fu fu fu...!``
``...Um, ...excuse me, I'm Rena Ryuuguu.@k@vS12/02/180200584. May I ask a question?``

``I don't understand the logic that says the dam war being over means we shouldn't file our appeal with the child consultation center.@k@vS12/02/180200586. Could you explain it in terms we can understand?``
Rena spoke with the calm composure of a neutral third party.
...But the flames of anger in the depths of her eyes could not be hidden.
I was probably the only one who noticed them.
To the elders, she must have looked like an easier opponent to deal with than the combative me.

``Reina-chan.@k@vS12/00/eldA18008. Now that the dam war is over, the village has no reason to fight the government.@k@vS12/00/eldA18009. Sure, we butted heads during the conflict.@k@vS12/00/eldA18010. But once the war ended, all that was water under the bridge.@k@vS12/00/eldA18011. The village made peace with the government.``

``Hinamizawa isn't its own country.@k@vS12/00/eldA18013. It's just a tiny village within Japan.@k@vS12/00/eldA18014. We have to keep going with the government's support.@k@vS12/00/eldA18015. That's why we need to stay on good terms with them.``
``...You're right.@k@vS12/02/180200588. I think maintaining a good relationship with the government is important.``
At least Reina-chan can have a proper, levelheaded conversation.@k Unlike the Maebara boy and his father!
I could hear murmurs to that effect.
``Right.@k@vS12/03/180300562. So basically, ever since the dam war ended, Hinamizawa has been pursuing a cooperative approach to repair its relationship with the government.``
``That's right, just like Mion-chan says!``
The old men voiced their agreement.@k But Shion immediately picked up where things left off.
``And as a result, the town council and the government are now thick as thieves.@k@vS12/06/180600327. Two government liaisons even tag along on the town council's annual hot spring trip.``

``Officially they're listed as personal attendees, but they claim travel expenses, so it's very much official business.@k@vS12/06/180600329. And naturally, most of the trip's costs are covered by government subsidies.@k@vS12/06/180600330. That's not all, is it?``

``The New Year's party, the year-end party gifts, the massive funding for every single town council event.@k@vS12/06/180600332. Watanagashi is no exception -- the government subsidies alone amount to several million yen.``

``In other words, the government is footing nearly the entire bill for Watanagashi.@k@vS12/06/180600334. Ever since the dam war ended, the festival and its after-parties have been getting grander and grander.``
``Behind all that extravagance are the enormous subsidies from the government.@k@vS12/06/180600336. So if we fall out of the government's favor, Watanagashi might not happen at all next year.``
``That's why the current Hinamizawa is wagging its tail for the government. Isn't that right, Grandpa?``
``Sh-... Shion-chan...@k@vS12/18/183300027. How do you know all of that...?!``
``Ahaha, well, every now and then I happen to be Sis, you see.``

``...What the hell.@k@vS12/01/180101321. The more I listen, the more absurd this gets.@k@vS12/01/180101322. I still can't see a single valid reason for opposing what we're doing.@k@vS12/01/180101323. Rena, could you summarize this for me?@k@vS12/01/180101324. What are they actually saying?``
``Sure.@k@vS12/02/180200590. To put it simply, they're in bed with the government and can't afford to upset them.``
Rena delivered that bombshell with the most innocent expression on her face, and the assembly hall erupted into an uproar.
changeReason: New adds "with the most innocent expression" from Japanese 涼しそうな顔; old omits Rena's expression.

TextENGNew: Some groaned at having their sore spot exposed,@k some fumed that we were speaking from ignorance,@k and some simply couldn't bear the indignity of being lectured by children. The reactions were varied.
Layer them all together, and you got a cacophony of angry murmuring and jostling.
``Quiet! Quiet!!``
The room only settled down when Mayor Kimiyoshi shouted them into silence.
By that point, the mayor's expression had become quite fearsome.

``Keiichi-kun, the relationship between the government and the town council is an entirely separate issue.@k@vS12/18/183300030. You're doing nothing but derailing the discussion.@k@vS12/18/183300031. Muddying the waters like this serves no purpose!!``
``Listen, Hinamizawa won a great victory during the dam war, but at the same time, we were left with a strongly negative image -- that of a village prone to causing trouble.``

``Part of that was because we deliberately leveraged that image as a strategy back then.@k@vS12/18/183300034. But now that the dam war is over, that image has become a serious liability for Hinamizawa.@k@vS12/18/183300035. We need to work on dispelling it.``

``If we don't, it will inflict lasting damage on this village's future -- this is based on a long-term outlook.@k@vS12/18/183300037. Do you understand?!@k@vS12/18/183300038. This is not some petty squabble about enemies and allies!``
``In other words...@k@vS12/03/180300564. You're saying that if Kei-chan rallies everyone, it'll ruin the friendly atmosphere you've painstakingly built up.``
The old men nodded vigorously at that, murmuring their agreement.
They seemed to think Mion was on their side.
``Mii-chan.@k@vS12/02/180200592. What 'friendly atmosphere'?@k@vS12/02/RE082102. Are you saying we shouldn't speak up for a child of this village who's suffering and in pain?``
``To put it plainly, they're saying the consultation center is doing its job at its own pace.@k@vS12/06/180600340. So they'd appreciate it if we stopped meddling.``

``The Okinomiya government can't come right out and tell us to stop, given their position.@k@vS12/06/SHIO082103. So since they've been doing all sorts of favors for the village, they're asking you to handle it internally. That's what this comes down to.``
``So what you're saying is... the Hinamizawa town council is basically the government's lapdog.``
``Watch your mouth, boy!!@k@vS12/00/eldB18004. What kind of way is that to talk!!``
``You're the ones who need to choose your words carefully!!@k@vS12/01/180101327. Don't tell me... it's not because Satoko is a daughter of the hated Houjou family, is it?``

``That's not it at all!@k@vS12/18/183300040. We're not saying anything about Satoko-chan!@k@vS12/18/183300041. We're telling you to look at the bigger picture and consider scaling back your appeals a bit.``

``The consultation center isn't ignoring you.@k@vS12/18/183300043. They're giving the fullest consideration to the passion you've shown by marching in there day after day with all those people, you know?!``
``What you want to convey has already been conveyed clearly enough.@k@vS12/18/183300045. Everyone is saying that pushing any further won't accomplish anything -- you've gone too far...!``
``...Keiichi-kun.@k@vS12/02/180200595. I just found something really interesting to read.``
``Look, over there -- the calligraphy in that frame.``
Rena pointed up at the wall.@k Everyone's gaze followed her finger.
The walls of the assembly hall were covered with framed pictures and documents.
Most of them were mementos from the dam war era, celebrating the Onigafuchi Guardians.
What Rena was pointing at was something none other than Mayor Kimiyoshi himself had written, back when he was chairman of the Guardians during the dam war.

``The middle part is especially interesting.@k@vS12/02/180200598_1. '...We sought peaceful and democratic dialogue, but the government and its @bかいらい.@<puppet@>, the president of the electric company, refused.'``
``'With unspeakable cruelty and injustice, they trampled upon the democratic movement of our villagers and the homeland of Hinamizawa.'``
``......Mii.@k@vS12/05/180500855. That is what Kimiyoshi wrote for the dam war victory memorial.@k@vS12/05/180500856. It is also inscribed on the monument.``
Everyone understood exactly why Rena had brought it up.
A sound like the collective swallowing of bitter medicine rose from the crowd.
changeReason: Old says "them all gulping"; new says "collective swallowing of bitter medicine" -- adds metaphor from Japanese 苦い唾.

TextENGNew: ``'We sought peaceful dialogue, but the government and its @bかいらい.@<puppet@>, the president of the electric company'...@k@vS12/01/180101329. Wait, what's this?@k@vS12/01/180101330. Shouldn't 'the electric company' be replaced with 'the Hinamizawa town council'?``
``'Puppet' is a pretty difficult word though. I bet some of the people here don't even know what it means.@k@vS12/01/180101332. Someone care to explain?``

``A @bかいらい.@<puppet@> originally refers to a marionette.@k@vS12/22/181400144. By extension, it describes a situation where an organization that is nominally independent is in fact doing the bidding of another entity.``
``Huh, that sounds like the perfect word for Hinamizawa right now, doesn't it, Sis?``
``...Ahahaha, yeah, it really does.``

``What nonsense!!@k@vS12/00/eldB18006. If you call cooperation and goodwill being a @bかいらい.@<puppet@>, then there's no room for kindness or consideration!!@k@vS12/00/eldB18007. That's not being a @bかいらい.@<puppet@> -- that's what you call sophistry!!``
``You just answered that yourselves.@k@vS12/01/180101334. You just clearly stated that your reason for telling us to stop has nothing to do with Satoko -- it's a village issue.``

``And you also said that 'village issue' means maintaining a friendly relationship with the government.@k@vS12/01/180101336. Don't you dare claim you don't remember saying that!!@k@vS12/01/180101337. You definitely said it!!@k@vS12/01/180101338. Right?!``
``Yes, I heard him.@k@vS12/02/180200601. He said the reason had nothing to do with Satoko-chan.``
``Mion.@k@vS12/01/180101340. Doesn't Hinamizawa have a saying about the whole village uniting for the sake of one person?``

``'If one throws a stone at you, two shall throw stones back.@k@vS12/03/180300567. If two throw stones at you, four shall throw stones.@k@vS12/03/180300568. If eight chase you with sticks, sixteen shall drive them away.@k@vS12/03/180300569. And if a thousand stand against you, the whole village shall rise to fight.'``

``'The humiliation suffered by one is suffered by all.@k@vS12/03/180300571. All shall unite for the sake of one villager.@k@vS12/03/180300572. That is the unbreakable solidarity of the Guardians.@k@vS12/03/180300573. The bond of the alliance is harder than stone, impervious even to the dam that holds back the waters.'``
``...I believe the latter portion was written by the chairman of the alliance at the time.``
The chairman of the Onigafuchi Guardians -- that was none other than the village mayor himself.
He folded his arms and began to groan.
He had a mountain of things he wanted to say, but couldn't find a single retort.@k That was the impression he gave.

``Since I moved here, Mion and Shion have told me all sorts of stirring stories about the dam war.@k@vS12/01/180101342. But that unity didn't start just five years ago with the dam war -- it wasn't some makeshift thing.``

``It's a burning tradition nurtured over more than forty years, forged in the hardship of the postwar era!!@k@vS12/01/180101344. That's why I find the people who cultivated that unity since those days truly incredible -- and worthy of my deepest respect!``
``And above all else, the valor of the Guardians honored in these frames on the walls is something truly magnificent!!``

``Back then, the government used every dirty trick in the book to try and sink Hinamizawa beneath a reservoir.@k@vS12/01/180101347. But you fought them all off!!@k@vS12/01/180101348. It's because men like you fought that Hinamizawa still stands today!``

``Without you, this place would be at the bottom of a lake right now!!@k@vS12/01/180101350. So I want to give thanks that this village still exists -- a village that can take pride in the beauty of its land and the unity of its people!@k@vS12/01/180101351. And I want to be proud of the fact that I get to live in this Hinamizawa!!``
``Damn right!``
Someone shouted.
It wasn't one of my friends.
It was one of the old men.
``That's exactly why -- that's why we...!!@k@vS12/01/180101353. Because we live in this very Hinamizawa, we can't help but follow that example!!``

``If one throws a stone at you, two shall throw stones back.@k@vS12/01/180101355. If two throw stones at you, four shall throw stones.@k@vS12/01/180101356. If eight chase you with sticks, sixteen shall drive them away.@k@vS12/01/180101357. And if a thousand stand against you, the whole village shall rise to fight.``

``The humiliation suffered by one is suffered by all.@k@vS12/01/180101359. All shall unite for the sake of one villager.@k@vS12/01/180101360. That is the unbreakable solidarity of the Guardians!!@k@vS12/01/180101361. Houjou Satoko is one of this village!!``
``And you all know far better than I do what a monster that uncle of hers is.``
changeReason: New adds "far better than I do" qualifier matching Japanese 俺よりみんなの方がよく知っている that old omitted.

TextENGNew: ``So lend us the power of the Guardians once more!@k@vS12/01/180101366. Reunite the spirit of the disbanded Guardians one more time!!!``
Rena and Mion began to applaud, and it quickly spread through the entire room.
On the faces of the old men who had been opposing me moments ago, I could see smiles -- as if they had reclaimed some lost dignity.
changeReason: New adds "as if they had regained their dignity" matching Japanese 尊厳を取り戻した that old omitted.

TextENGNew: But those smiles faded quickly, and they all began whispering with the people next to them.
``Keiichi-kun is right.@k@vS12/00/eldA18017. Doing as the government says isn't what Hinamizawa is about!``
``But the dam war's over now...@k@vS12/00/eldD18003. We owe the government a lot, too...``
``I had no idea.@k@vS12/00/eldE18002. Is our town council really that cozy with the government?``
``Well... personally, I think the Maebara boy is right.``
``I don't like how the town council's been handling things.@k@vS12/00/eldH18002. Keiichi-kun is right.@k@vS12/00/eldH18003. Pressuring him because the government told us to -- that's disgraceful!``
``Mr. Mayor.@k@vS12/00/eldI18002. ...Even if we can't actively support Maebara-kun, asking him to stand down doesn't sit right with me.``
``I don't like this Maebara kid's mouth!@k@vS12/00/eldC18007. He's got no respect for his elders whatsoever!!``
``You think you can get your way talking to us like that?!``
Three particularly fierce-looking old men crowded around me, each shouting over the other.
changeReason: New adds "each shouting over the other" matching Japanese 口々に怒鳴った; old just says "came up to yell."

TextENGNew: But I shouted right back at all of them, louder than all three combined.

``This isn't about how I talk!@k@vS12/01/180101368. It's about the soul of this village!!@k@vS12/01/180101369. If you don't have the spine to stand up for one of your own, then sit down and shut up!!``
``Wh-What did you just say, you impudent brat!!``
WHACK!
A particularly burly old man smacked me on the head.
``What the hell do you think you're doing!!!``
WHACK!
I smacked him right back, and the whole room descended into pandemonium.
Rena came over.
I assumed she was coming to break things up, but to my surprise, Rena smacked the old man on the head without a moment's hesitation.
``Wha?!@k@vS12/00/eldG18003. What do you think you're doing?!``
``Don't think I'll go easy on you just because you're a girl!!!``
Rena got whacked on the head too.
But she didn't flinch in the slightest and talked right back with a resolute expression.
``If one person gets hit, two hit back.@k@vS12/02/180200603. Isn't that the spirit of Hinamizawa?!``
``And since you hit Rena-san, that means four of us need to respond.@k@vS12/10/181000296. Allow me to stand as well.``

``Keiichi.@k@vS12/00/182600061. Your father is standing up too!@k@vS12/00/182600062. What you're saying is right.@k@vS12/00/182600063. If they try to silence you with violence, I will show your enemies no mercy!``
``......It appears there are three people blocking Keiichi's path.@k@vS12/05/180500858. Then we need six to stand against them.``
``R-Rika-chama...!!``
``That's quite spirited of you, Rika-chama.@k@vS12/06/180600345. Count me in too!``
``...I, too, will stand with Maebara-kun.``
Chie-sensei rose to her feet, timidly but resolutely.
changeReason: New adds "timidly but resolutely" matching Japanese おずおずと; old just says "stood up."

TextENGNew: I had thought she'd given in under the pressure.

``I nearly abandoned my student in the name of 'the bigger picture.'@k@vS12/22/181400147. ...I told myself I would sacrifice anything for my students, yet I prioritized my own precarious position instead.``
``I am ashamed of myself!``
As she spoke, Chie-sensei fixed the mayor with a piercing stare.
...It was her unmistakable answer to the man who had just pressured her.
``I may have overstepped my bounds as an educator, it's true.@k@vS12/22/181400150. But what Maebara-kun is doing to save Satoko-chan is absolutely the right thing.``
``As an educator, I have a duty to affirm that what is right is right.@k@vS12/22/181400152. That is why I cannot bring myself to tell Maebara-kun to stop!``

``If I've even got my teacher backing me up, then nobody can hold me back!@k@vS12/01/180101373. All right, I'll ask everyone one more time.@k@vS12/01/180101374. Which matters more -- cozying up to the government, or saving one of your own villagers?! Make it clear!!``

``Mr. Mayor.@k@vS12/03/180300576. What Kei-chan is doing embodies the very soul of Hinamizawa.@k@vS12/03/180300577. His determination to fight without yielding to any opposition is the very spirit of the Onigafuchi Guardians.``
``I believe it's shameful for this town council to ask him to show restraint.@k@vS12/03/180300579. If you still insist on silencing him...``
``...I will smash every last one of these memorial photographs in their frames and burn the banner of the Guardians right here and now!``
While the mayor could do nothing but groan, the senior members began to speak up.
``I support Maebara-kun.@k@vS12/00/eldA18019. His mouth may be rough, but his spirit is true!``
``I agree!@k@vS12/00/eldD18005. Getting along with the government is one thing, but groveling before them? That's truly shameful!``
``Keiichi-kun, don't worry about a thing -- keep fighting!@k@vS12/00/eldE18004. Don't give in to their pressure!``
Someone clapped their hands powerfully, and a great wave of applause instantly filled the assembly hall.
``Maebara-kun, since we're all here, why don't you fill us in on the situation so far?``

``Certainly.@k@vS12/01/KEI082103. On the very first day Satoko's uncle returned, Chie-sensei immediately visited the Houjou household.@k@vS12/01/180101377. But he refused to let her see Satoko, and the situation was clearly abnormal.@k@vS12/01/180101378. Chie-sensei prioritized her student's safety and immediately contacted the consultation center.``

``That evening, a case worker from the center visited and conducted an interview.@k@vS12/03/MIO082102. They concluded there was no evidence of a problem and decided to take a wait-and-see approach.@k@vS12/03/MIO082103. It might be called a cautious response, but given the circumstances, I don't believe they properly grasped the situation.``
``So the next day, led by Kei-chan, the five of us went to the consultation center to demand urgent action.``
``But they said they wanted to observe the situation carefully and wouldn't take us seriously.``
``We decided that five people wasn't enough to make our point, so the following day we went back with more than twenty -- our entire class.``
``You mobilized the whole class...?``
``Kei-chan gave a speech for the ages.@k@vS12/06/180600347. 'Save your friend! Don't abandon her!' Everyone got fired up!``
``If Kei-chan had been around during the dam war, I bet his charisma could have ended it a year sooner!``
``Ha ha ha! With the spirit that Maebara boy's got, I can believe it!``
``But even on the second day, they didn't really listen to us.@k@vS12/01/180101380. Almost no progress.@k@vS12/01/180101381. All they said was that they were 'handling it carefully.'``
``Actually, Keiichi-kun.@k@vS12/00/eldA18022. You did make an impact.@k@vS12/00/eldA18023. They were so rattled that they went through the local government to check in with our town council.``
``The fact that they started applying pressure means your pressure was getting to them.@k@vS12/00/eldD18007. You were making progress!``
I see... so you could look at it that way too.
If they didn't take us seriously at all, they wouldn't have bothered with the pressure!

``So on the third day -- today -- we called on friends and acquaintances and showed up sixty strong.@k@vS12/01/180101383. This time, the head of the consultation center agreed to speak with us personally.``
``I believe he listened to what we had to say, but he avoided giving a direct answer on what action they'd take.``
``Keiichi-kun, the truth is...@k@vS12/00/toshi082102. by that point, the council had already spoken with the officials, and we had decided to ask you to stand down.``
``Once the consultation center confirmed that the village wasn't backing you, they planned to simply brush off your appeals.``

``...That figures.@k@vS12/10/IRI08011. I could sense it from the way they responded to us.@k@vS12/10/IRI08012. There was a subtle air about them -- as if everything had already been settled behind the scenes and they just needed to get through the meeting.``

``We're planning to go again tomorrow, of course, but... I wonder if they'll even listen.@k@vS12/02/RE082104. Since the Okinomiya consultation center thinks the village has handled things internally, they probably won't bother with us anymore...``
``Exactly.@k@vS12/06/180600350. They believe the town council is on their side.``
``......The town council needs to make its support for Keiichi clear, or we are in trouble.``
``Indeed.@k@vS12/10/181000301. Otherwise, any future appeals will simply be turned away at the door.``
``Come on, Mayor!@k@vS12/00/eldA18028. Just tell them outright that Hinamizawa won't interfere with what Maebara-kun is doing!``
``.........Hmmmm...@k@vS12/18/183300053. Personally, I don't want to stand in Keiichi-kun's way either...``

``...But from the town council's standpoint... it's delicate...@k@vS12/18/183300055. Even declaring neutrality would be tantamount to siding with Keiichi-kun given the current situation.@k@vS12/18/183300056. It'd be no different from an outright endorsement.``
``...But I still need to consult the full council...@k@vS12/18/183300058. I can't decide this alone...@k@vS12/18/183300059. ...I need unanimous agreement from all the council members...``
``Most of the council members are already here today, Grandpa.``
``Let's just lay all our cards on the table.@k@vS12/01/180101386. ...You're afraid there are people among you who object to taking the side of a Houjou girl, aren't you?``
Everyone fell silent.
This was the root of the problem, hidden behind all the pretty words.
changeReason: Old says "heart of the matter"; new adds "hidden behind all the pretty words" from Japanese 綺麗事の陰.

TextENGNew: ``Everyone claims the dam war is over.@k@vS12/01/180101388. You all said that to me just now.@k@vS12/01/180101389. And logically, the prejudice against the Houjou family should have ended with it.``
``But some of that prejudice has been dragging on, hasn't it?``
``You're all still afraid of what rumors might spread if anyone were seen having contact with a Houjou.``
``Satoko-chan's parents may have been the leaders of the pro-relocation faction, but what sin did Satoko-chan herself commit?``
``......Personally, I have nothing against Satoko-chan.@k@vS12/00/eldC18011. But some others might...``
``I don't have anything against her either.@k@vS12/00/eldG18005. The dam war's over, and with the accident, there's already been a certain... reckoning...``
``...I don't hold anything against her either.@k@vS12/00/eldH18005. But there are still people around who can't stand the Houjous...``

``Let me say something.@k@vS12/06/180600353. If anyone here hates Satoko, please raise your hand.@k@vS12/06/180600354. I'm certain no one will.@k@vS12/06/180600355. ...And yet, everyone believes they'll be pointed at and whispered about if they get involved with Satoko of the Houjou family.``
``But here's the thing -- those people don't exist!@k@vS12/06/180600357. There's nobody like that!``
``No... that can't be right.@k@vS12/00/eldG18007. There are people like that out there.``
``Then give me a name.@k@vS12/06/180600359. And don't tell me you heard it from a friend of a friend.``
No one spoke.
It had become painfully obvious that the village's vague aversion toward Satoko was nothing but an outdated misunderstanding.

``At this rate, nothing we do will make a difference.@k@vS12/01/180101392. The consultation center believes the town council is on their side.@k@vS12/01/180101393. ...Unless the town council makes its position clear, we can't continue this fight.``
``......Kimiyoshi.@k@vS12/05/180500861. Could it be that... you need a certain someone's permission?``
``............Ouch.@k@vS12/18/183300063. ...Rika-chama always knows right where it hurts...``
The mayor forced a pained smile.
I had no idea what Rika-chan was referring to, but she'd clearly hit the mark.@k But who? Who was this person?
Who was this 'certain someone'?!
If we could persuade them, the town council would be on our side!
``Could it be... the old hag?``
changeReason: Old says "my hag of a grandmother" (specifying relationship); new says "the old hag." Japanese 鬼婆 is just "demon hag."

TextENGNew: ``......The Kimiyoshi family considers its grudge against the Houjou family water under the bridge.@k@vS12/18/183300065. The Furude family feels the same way, I assume?``
``......Mii.@k@vS12/05/180500863. I am also a head of the Three Families.``
Hinamizawa placed great importance on consensus among the Three Families.
That tradition dated all the way back to the era of Onigafuchi Village.
...Such traditions may have been musty and outdated, but if this was a road we had to travel to gain greater power, then so be it.
changeReason: New adds conditional "if this was a road we had to travel to gain..." matching fuller Japanese sentence.

TextENGNew: ``In that case, all that's left is the head of the Sonozaki family.@k@vS12/01/180101395. ...I hear they're the most powerful of the three and effectively run the show?``
That was something everyone knew but no one dared say aloud.@k Only someone like me, free of those entanglements, could put it into words.
``...So if we can convince Mii-chan's grandmother, the Hinamizawa town council will publicly declare its support for us?``
``.............What do you say, Mion-chan...?``
``...Well, I'd rather not fight with Granny if I can help it.``
``But if everything else has been settled and Granny is the only thing standing between us and saving Satoko, then I'll fight.``
changeReason: New renders as direct speech matching Japanese dialogue format; old paraphrases.

TextENGNew: ``Not as the next head of the Sonozaki family.@k@vS12/03/180300589. I'll fight as the president of our club -- as Satoko's class representative -- as her friend.``
``Kimiyoshi-san.@k@vS12/01/180101397. Promise me.@k@vS12/01/180101398. If we persuade Mion's grandmother, the town council will back us, right?``
``Of course.@k@vS12/18/183300068. If the heads of all Three Families are in agreement, no one in the council will object.``
``...So to sum it up, every last one of you here is saying no because you're scared of Mion's grandmother.@k@vS12/01/180101400. You're all grown adults, and you're pathetic!``
``Easy for you to say, Maebara boy...@k@vS12/00/eldA18031. Oryou-san is truly terrifying, you know...?``

``Ahahaha, that's not true at all.@k@vS12/02/180200616. I know Mii-chan's grandmother very well.@k@vS12/02/180200617. Sure, she has a scary side -- I know that firsthand -- but she's really not that bad.@k@vS12/02/180200618. Keiichi-kun when he's angry is twice as scary.``

``And an angry Rena is twice as scary as me.@k@vS12/01/180101402. All right, it's decided.@k@vS12/01/180101403. We'll persuade Mion's grandmother ourselves!!@k@vS12/01/180101404. And when we do, you'd better support us.``
``Actually, scratch that -- 'support' is too tame. After the fight we just put up here.@k@vS12/01/180101406. I expect your full cooperation!!``
``C-Cooperation? What kind of cooperation...?!``

``He's saying the Onigafuchi Guardians should reunite and fight together.@k@vS12/10/181000303. Surely you don't intend to leave a protest movement bearing the Guardians' name entirely in the hands of children?``
``You want us to march too...?!@k@vS12/00/eldA18033. That's going a bit far...``
``Of course we should!@k@vS12/00/eldD18010. We're going to declare to the government that we stand against them.@k@vS12/00/eldD18011. If we're doing this, we go all the way!``

``...If we support Keiichi-kun under the name of the Hinamizawa United Town Council, we need to make our stance absolutely clear.@k@vS12/00/eldE18007. Declaring support without actually cooperating would be inconsistent.``

``Once the town council declares its support, this is no longer Maebara-kun's fight alone.@k@vS12/00/eldF18003. It becomes the entire council's fight.@k@vS12/00/eldF18004. And if that's the case, the representative should be the mayor himself!@k@vS12/00/eldF18005. The mayor must personally go to the government and protest!``
``......Fine, fine, I understand!``

``If Oryou-san gives her consent, then I'll show some backbone!@k@vS12/18/183300072. I'll lend Keiichi-kun my full support!!@k@vS12/18/183300073. Everyone from the council gathered here today -- are we in agreement?!``
``......But you are leaving the task of persuading Oryou to Keiichi, are you not?``
``Ahahahaha... ahh... fine, fine.@k@vS12/18/183300075. I'll accompany him when he goes to persuade Oryou-san.``
``Keiichi-kun's manner of speaking is a bit too youthful, after all.@k@vS12/18/183300077. ...Oryou-san needs someone who can speak to her in the language of her generation.``
``I apologize for all the terrible things I said today.@k@vS12/01/180101408. I'm truly sorry, everyone.``

``No!@k@vS12/00/eldC18014. What you said needed to be said!@k@vS12/00/eldC18015. You cut right through the council's collusion!@k@vS12/00/eldC18016. Your refusal to yield to pressure is the very pride of Hinamizawa!``
``Sorry for hitting you earlier.@k@vS12/00/eldG18009. I lost my temper. I apologize.``
``No, I should be the one apologizing...``
``Well said, Chie-sensei!@k@vS12/00/eldE18010. You're a fine teacher!``
``...But to think there are still people holding a grudge against the Houjou family...``
``Well... I'd say Oryou-san is the one who dislikes them the most...``
But according to Mion, even Oryou-san didn't actually hate the Houjou family anymore.@k ...What a ridiculous situation!
``Nfu fu fu!@k@vS12/11/180700314. Not bad at all, Maebara-san.@k@vS12/11/180700315. I never imagined you'd win over the town council.``
``Well, I haven't won them over just yet.@k@vS12/01/180101411. It's conditional on persuading Mion's grandmother.``

``Oryou-san may hold a dictatorial position, but she can't afford to lose the council's backing.@k@vS12/11/180700317. If the groundwork with the town council has already been laid, it would be difficult for the Sonozaki family alone to oppose you.``
``My, my, you're quite the skilled negotiator.@k@vS12/11/180700319. Especially the technique of tearing them down at the start and then building them back up -- masterful!``
``Oh, well... I just tried to do it the way my dad writes his art magazine columns.@k@vS12/01/180101413. His columns are basically the same style...``

``Oh?@k@vS12/11/180700321. Maebara-san's father seems to have set a fine example for his son.@k@vS12/11/180700322. He's an artist, isn't he?@k@vS12/11/180700323. I'd love to see some of his work sometime.``
``Oh, well, ha ha ha ha......``
My dad draws the kind of pictures he can't show his own son, and apparently he can't show them to a police detective either.@k ...That only deepened my suspicions about how questionable they were.

``Then let's pay a visit to Oryou-san tomorrow evening.@k@vS12/18/183300079. Showing up tonight on such short notice wouldn't be wise.@k@vS12/18/183300080. Once Oryou-san is in a bad mood, she won't listen to reason no matter how sound it is...``
``The ones going will be me and the mayor.@k@vS12/03/180300591. Who else?``
``I'm going too, of course.@k@vS12/06/180600362. Rena-san says she'll come as well.@k@vS12/06/180600363. She says if Granny doesn't nod agreeably, she'll split her head open.``
``Hau! I would never do that!@k@vS12/02/180200620. Shii-chan, that's mean!``
``......Keiichi, I am going too.@k@vS12/05/180500866. I will have words with Oryou.``
``Good, good.@k@vS12/01/180101415. Give her a piece of your mind!!@k@vS12/01/180101416. I'll certainly be giving her a piece of mine!``
``...I'll find a way to make time as well.@k@vS12/10/181000305. Even just being there can lend some weight to your cause, I think.``
``Well, well, things are getting interesting.@k@vS12/06/180600365. So, Sis -- are you ready to go up against the old hag?``
``......Honestly, I kind of wish you'd swap places with me for tomorrow.@k@vS12/03/180300593. Ahahaha...``
``Keiichi-kun, things have really snowballed, haven't they?``

``Yeah.@k@vS12/01/180101418. The more people come together, the stronger that power becomes.@k@vS12/01/180101419. If all of Hinamizawa stands united, we can save Satoko in no time!@k@vS12/01/180101420. After all, these are the people who went toe to toe with the national government and won!``
Whenever someone brought up the dam war, the old men couldn't help but feel a swell of pride.
Before long, everyone was eagerly swapping tales of their heroic exploits from those days.
``......The dead-end fate that said Satoko could never be saved... I feel as though it's on the verge of being shattered.@k@vS12/05/180500868. .........The force behind it is this overwhelming.``
``The power to break fate is nothing like a mere string of small lucky breaks, each whispering 'maybe this time will work'... It is something incomparably, overwhelmingly greater...``
